meta.analyst: Opus senior (Phase 2 + Phase 2 re-judgement per Phase 5 HIGH finding, §9.3 routing)
meta.evidence_note: All code path:line evidence verified by direct file reads against the current working tree. SRS path:line references point to docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md (line numbers from requirements-full.json block/section/AC/trace fields).
rejudgement_corrections: FR-BGSTAB-019: relabeled server/config.json5:21-24 as an addition_site (session block; processCleanup override to be added here, confirmed ABSENT by direct read) rather than supporting evidence, and added server/src/schemas/config.schema.ts:84-89 (sessionProcessCleanupSchema, mode default 'observe') as the actual observe-default evidence.

All four original senior REQs (FR-BGSTAB-019, OBS-BGSTAB-001, PERF-BGSTAB-001, REL-BGSTAB-005): populated evidence path:line on the five non-implementability axes (evidence_strength, dependency_health, ac_verifiability, scope_fit, product_fit) which were previously empty.
Added formal 6-axis judgements for FR-BGSTAB-015/016/017/018 (previously prescreen quick_pass with midpoints 77-78 straddling the high/medium boundary).

implementability.rationale: Enforce mechanism is already built and verified; the change is a config activation + TDD test + soak gate, not new mechanism. The enforce branch runs the verified process-tree terminator with root-process metadata, and mode is read from runtimeSessionConfig.processCleanup so a config.json5 override flows straight through. config.json5 currently has no session.processCleanup block (session block holds only idleDelayMs/runningDelayMs), so the addition_site is a small localized insertion. Code difficulty is very low; the only real cost is the operational >=2h multi-session soak (AC-3) that must record zero remaining verified-owned descendants before commit.
evidence: server/config.json5:21-24 (addition_site: session block; processCleanup override to be added here — confirmed ABSENT, not current supporting evidence)

evidence_strength.rationale: All code trace paths exist and match the statement. SessionManager.ts:1149-1171 is the confirmed enforce-mode termination branch; processTreeTerminator.ts:592-605 is the confirmed Windows PID-only taskkill.exe /PID /T /F (tree kill, no broad /IM name kill); config.schema.ts:84-89 confirms the schema default 'observe'; config.json5:21-24 is a valid addition_site (no processCleanup key present yet, confirmed by direct read). trace_validation reports all_paths_exist=true, no mismatches.

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-011 (implemented/evolving) and depends_on FR-BGSTAB-009 (implemented/evolving) — both healthy, both already implemented. No draft dependencies, no cycles. Dependency graph is clean for this REQ.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (config resolves enforce while schema default stays observe), AC-2 (identity-validated termination, no broad name-kill), AC-5 (TDD red/green regression) are deterministic and unit-testable. AC-4 has a documentation deliverable ('rollback path is documented') alongside a verifiable mechanism. AC-3 is objective but is a >=2h operational soak run rather than an automated test, which caps the axis.
evidence: server/src/test-runner.ts (test addition_site for enforce-activation + soak-gate regression)

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB session-cleanup / process-tree termination — squarely inside buildergate-stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Core to the 54-concurrent-shell goal: activating enforce cleanup prevents session-owned descendant process leaks at multi-session scale, which is a direct stability requirement for native-scale operation.

conditions: AC-3 soak gate: a >=2h multi-session soak recording zero remaining verified-owned descendants must pass before the enforce activation is committed.
AC-4: an observe rollback path must remain available and be documented (config revert restores prior behavior without code changes).
AC-5: TDD red-first regression test (operational config resolves mode=enforce while schema default remains observe) authored before the config change.
Activate enforce via a config.json5 override only; must not change config.schema.ts default (observe) or FR-BGSTAB-009 AC-1 schema-omission fallback.

implementability.rationale: Substantial cross-cutting observability build across 5 addition sites spanning server and frontend, including two very high-complexity modules (WsRouter.ts ~2409 LOC/180 branches, SessionManager.ts ~3569 LOC/287 branches) and two large React components (TerminalContainer.tsx ~1878 LOC, TerminalView.tsx ~2293 LOC). New telemetry event + protocol fields, transport/replay counters, snapshot/repair timing correlation, and a recovery-write-gate instrument. It also depends on four planned requirements (FR-BGSTAB-015/016/017, REL-BGSTAB-003), so several of the behaviors it must observe do not exist yet, coupling delivery to those. Feasible but broad and sequencing-sensitive.

evidence_strength.rationale: All 5 code trace paths exist (trace_validation all_paths_exist=true, no mismatches) and land in the correct regions (WsRouter transport/replay observability, ws-protocol telemetry block, SessionManager snapshot/repair, frontend recovery gates). Statement and ACs are consistent with the addition sites.

dependency_health.rationale: extends REL-BGSTAB-001 (implemented/evolving, healthy) but also carries a STALE extends link to REL-BGSTAB-002 (discarded). Verified against the SRS body: OBS-BGSTAB-001's statement and ACs concern telemetry/redaction/redraw-flood/soak; the only REL-BGSTAB-002-flavored content (AC-5 remainingVerifiedDescendants=0 shutdown-cleanup soak evidence) now lives in the successor chain REL-BGSTAB-004 (implemented) / REL-BGSTAB-005 (supersedes REL-BGSTAB-002), so the scope basis still exists but the link points at a discarded node. Also depends_on four planned/evolving REQs (FR-BGSTAB-015/016/017, REL-BGSTAB-003). No draft deps, no cycles.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (named counters recorded), AC-2 (raw-payload redaction), AC-3 (deterministic redraw-flood asserts queue limits/starvation/ordering/input release rather than human-perceived slowness) are verifiable via shape/redaction/deterministic tests. AC-4 is a verifiable procedural constraint (localhost:2222, health 2221, start.bat/stop.bat, no broad node kill). AC-5 (final E2E + soak: loss/reorder/dup=0, input_write_failed=0, remainingVerifiedDescendants=0, counters back to baseline) is objective but operationally heavy. ACs were refined through prior sub-agent eval (3 change-note refinements). Caps at 13 due to the E2E/soak operational dependency.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB ConPTY observability/telemetry — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Directly supports the 54-session native-performance goal by making ConPTY input-lag / queue-pressure bottlenecks measurable (measurement precedes optimization). Slightly below perfect because it is a diagnostic enabler rather than the throughput fix itself, though the REQ explicitly frames measurable evidence as the completion criterion since the slowness is hard to reproduce.
evidence: docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1519 (Rationale: slowness hard to reproduce, judge via measurable bottlenecks)

conditions: Re-validate/re-point the stale extends link to the discarded REL-BGSTAB-002; the shutdown-cleanup-soak-evidence basis moved to REL-BGSTAB-004/REL-BGSTAB-005 (supersedes), so the link is traceability hygiene and does not block statement/AC realization but should be corrected.
Sequence after FR-BGSTAB-015/016/017 and REL-BGSTAB-003 (all planned) — the observed behaviors must exist before their telemetry can be fully asserted.
AC-5 requires a deterministic ConPTY redraw-flood E2E at localhost:2222 plus a soak run (operational validation).
AC-2 redaction constraint: no durable storage of raw terminal input/output, snapshot, or ANSI patch content in telemetry/soak artifacts.
judgements.external_module_impact: Adds instrumentation into two very high-complexity in-scope modules (WsRouter.ts ~2409 LOC/180 branches, SessionManager.ts ~3569 LOC/287 branches) and two large React components; additive observability, but regression surface in heavily-branched files is non-trivial.

implementability.rationale: Localized change in a small low-complexity module (terminalOutputScheduler.ts ~209 LOC/15 branches). The current flush() drains a single queue[0] entry per animation frame, already uses takeUtf8Prefix for code-point safety and a shouldYield input-yield hook — so converting to a time-budgeted multi-chunk drain loop (while budget-remaining && queue non-empty && !shouldYield) is a well-scoped internal modification that reuses existing safety primitives. The config.json5:79 override (16384) -> schema default (262144) is a one-line change, both values confirmed. Two design decisions remain: (a) AC-3's 'configurable per-frame time budget' (default 6-8ms) has no config-schema addition_site, so implementer must choose internal constant vs new config key; (b) the module is co-owned with FR-BGSTAB-017 (planned) which also mutates scheduler internals.

evidence_strength.rationale: terminalOutputScheduler.ts:52-105 confirmed as the single-chunk-per-frame flush region with existing code-point-safe split and input-yield check. config.json5:79 confirmed = visibleFlushBudgetBytes:16384 (matches AC-4 'previously set to 16384'). config.schema.ts:150 confirmed default 262144 (matches AC-4/verifies trace). Minor deduction: the statement's 'configurable per-frame time budget' has no corresponding config trace, a small statement/trace incompleteness.

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-003 (implemented/evolving) and depends_on FR-BGSTAB-001 (implemented/evolving) — both healthy. depends_on FR-BGSTAB-017 (planned/evolving) introduces same-file coordination coupling but no draft dependency and no cycle. Deduction reflects the one planned dependency plus the shared-module ordering constraint.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (multi-chunk drain within budget, no code-point split), AC-2 (input-yield stops draining), AC-3 (default budget within 6-8ms), AC-4 (effective budget = 262144, no throttling override), AC-5 (TDD red/green) are all unit-testable in the existing frontend/tests/unit/terminalOutputScheduler.test.ts harness. The 'AC-1: AC-1:' label duplication is cosmetic only and does not affect verifiability.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB xterm visible-output performance — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Rationale explicitly names the single-chunk cadence + under-provisioned 16384 override as 'a primary contributor to sluggish native performance at 54-session scale' — directly core to the native-performance goal.
evidence: docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1788 (Rationale: primary contributor to sluggish native performance at 54-session scale)

conditions: AC-3 'configurable per-frame time budget' (default 6-8ms) has no config-schema addition_site: implementer must decide internal constant vs new config key. If a config key is chosen, add config.schema.ts + config.types.ts + configTemplate.ts consistently.
Coordinate implementation order with FR-BGSTAB-017 (planned) — both change terminalOutputScheduler.ts internals; whichever lands first defines the base the other builds on.
Preserve FR-BGSTAB-003 callback-order and code-point-safety guarantees (do not split multibyte code points).
If restoring the byte budget by removing the config.json5 override rather than rewriting 262144, keep configTemplate.ts rendering consistent.

implementability.rationale: Core code change is well-scoped and addition sites are confirmed: terminateMultipleSessions (SessionManager.ts:1783-1810) is exactly the sequential for-of await loop to convert to a bounded-concurrency pool, and gracefulShutdown.ts:11 DEFAULT_SESSION_CLEANUP_TIMEOUT_MS=3000 is the fixed constant to rescale. The bounded-pool mechanism is standard. However three factors reduce implementability: (1) two design parameters ({{OQ-1}} concurrency limit, {{OQ-2}} timeout scaling formula) are undecided pending user decision; (2) AC-10 requires FR-BGSTAB-010 idempotent single finalization to hold under parallel termination, which is a genuine concurrency-correctness risk (natural PTY exit + shutdown termination racing to finalize the same session) that the current sequential loop sidesteps; (3) 12 ACs including a 54-session soak-completion criterion (AC-11).
evidence: server/src/services/SessionManager.ts:1795-1810 (the exact sequential await + telemetry aggregation to parallelize)
server/src/services/SessionManager.ts:1156-1170 (enforce termination + finalize path relevant to idempotency)

evidence_strength.rationale: gracefulShutdown.ts:11 confirmed (DEFAULT_SESSION_CLEANUP_TIMEOUT_MS = 3_000). SessionManager.ts:1783-1810 confirmed as the strictly sequential 'for (const id of ids) { await terminateSession(...) }' loop — exactly matching the addition_site note 'parallelize the sequential for-of await loop with bounded concurrency'. test-runner.ts:198-200 is a valid verifies anchor for existing shutdown/timeout tests. All paths exist, no mismatches.

dependency_health.rationale: supersedes REL-BGSTAB-002 (discarded) — this is the expected successor lifecycle pattern (discarded_ref_issues classifies it as 'expected', not stale). depends_on FR-BGSTAB-010 (implemented/evolving, healthy). BUT depends_on FR-BGSTAB-019 which is planned/DRAFT — dependency_health_risks explicitly flags this unhealthy edge, and REL-BGSTAB-005's own stability is also draft. No cycles. The draft dependency plus own-draft state is the main deduction.

ac_verifiability.rationale: 10 of 12 ACs are objective and testable (AC-1 stage order, AC-2 evidence fields, AC-3 daemon-stop no-broad-kill, AC-4/AC-5 evidence semantics, AC-6 invariant preservation, AC-7 honest reporting, AC-10 idempotency, AC-11 54-session completion, AC-12 TDD). However AC-8 embeds {{OQ-1}} (concurrency limit) and AC-9 embeds {{OQ-2}} (timeout scaling value/formula); ac_verifiability_risks flags that these cannot be objectively verified until the open questions are resolved. The structure is parameterized, so once OQ-1/OQ-2 are decided AC-8/AC-9 become deterministically testable — but they are currently un-verifiable, so 2 of 12 ACs are blocked.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB graceful-shutdown / multi-session session-cleanup — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Directly enables clean shutdown at 54-session scale (stated project goal): sequential termination plus a fixed 3000ms timeout does not complete cleanly at that scale. Core.
evidence: docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1911 (Rationale: fixed 3000ms + sequential does not complete at 54-session scale)

blockers: {{OQ-1}} (bounded concurrency limit) and {{OQ-2}} (session-count timeout scaling value/formula) are undecided open questions pending user decision. AC-8 and AC-9 cannot be fully specified or given deterministic regression tests until these are resolved; the parallel-batch and scaled-timeout behaviors are blocked on that decision (the requirement's other 10 ACs remain implementable).
conditions: Preserve FR-BGSTAB-010 idempotent single finalization under parallel termination (AC-10) — concurrency-correctness constraint: no double finalization/double-count when natural PTY exit and shutdown termination target the same session.
Preserve the exact observable shutdown stage order (AC-1) and the no-broad-process-name-kill guarantee (AC-3) restated from REL-BGSTAB-002 (AC-6).
Depends on FR-BGSTAB-019 (currently draft) — its stability should firm up in tandem given both share the enforce/cleanup path.
AC-11 requires 54-session-scale shutdown-completion evidence (operational validation).
AC-12: TDD red-first regression tests (bounded-parallel termination, idempotent single finalization under parallelism, scaled-timeout completion) authored before implementation.
judgements.external_module_impact: Parallelizes terminateMultipleSessions inside the highest-complexity in-scope module (SessionManager.ts ~3569 LOC/287 branches); introducing concurrency into finalization there carries real regression risk to idempotent single-finalization guarantees.

implementability.rationale: Large-surface but highly additive REQ that extends the already-shipped Wave6 Settings pipeline (RuntimeConfigStore capability map + SettingsService PATCH + config.schema + config.types + ConfigFileRepository + configTemplate + settingsDraftHelpers + SettingsPage UI). The RuntimeConfigStore capability map already contains many of the target fields (resourceLimits.ws.outputCoalesceWindowMs, perClientControlQueueMaxBytes, visibleFlushBudgetBytes confirmed present with applyScope/constraints), so the work is to unreserve/add the remaining ~28 matrix keys (recovery gate budgets, replay/repair limits, telemetry thresholds), add schema validation with cross-field relations (AC-3), and render 5 Settings UI groups. No new mechanism — the proven Wave6/Wave7 pattern applied at scale. The main cost is breadth (per-key schema + type + capability + UI + test) plus cross-field validation and the direct-mode safe-send dependency hint (AC-4).
evidence: server/src/services/RuntimeConfigStore.ts:48-124 (capability map; resourceLimits.ws/terminal entries already present at :74-87)

evidence_strength.rationale: All 8 code trace paths exist (prescreen trace_validation all_paths_exist=true, no mismatches). Directly confirmed: RuntimeConfigStore.ts:48-124 is the capability map with resourceLimits.ws/terminal entries, and config.schema.ts:118-199 is the resourceLimits schema region (terminalResourceLimitsSchema at :147). The SRS body carries an exhaustive per-key matrix (default/range/applyScope) consistent with the addition sites. This REQ also went through 8 sub-agent eval fix cycles (trace path corrections, matrix expansion).

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-001, FR-BGSTAB-008, FR-BGSTAB-013 — all three implemented/evolving and healthy. No draft dependencies, no cycles. Clean.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (matrix field exposed editable with constraints+applyScope), AC-2 (PATCH persistence + changedKeys/applySummary + immediate apply), AC-3 (validation rejects without silent clamping), AC-4 (safe-send dependency hint in direct mode), AC-5 (no-consumer fields stay read-only), AC-6 (exhaustive matrix coverage) are all testable via Settings capability tests + PATCH validation tests + UI unit/E2E. AC-6 is a long enumerated checklist but each key is individually verifiable. AC-4/AC-5 involve softer UI-conditional presentation. Caps at 13 for the UI-conditional and enumerated breadth.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB ConPTY Settings / runtime-config — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Foundational config substrate that makes every ConPTY tuning value editable — the ConPTY plan requires this before the transport/recovery/perf REQs (FR-016/017, PERF-001) that consume the Settings-backed limits. Core enabler, scored 9 because it is substrate rather than a direct native-performance gain by itself.
evidence: docs/research/2026-06-30.buildergate-conpty-output-recovery-input-lag-implementation-plan.md (Related Docs; plan requires all tuning values configurable)
docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1209 (Rationale: every new queue size/interval/timeout/threshold/mode configurable from Settings)

conditions: Add cross-field relationship validation (AC-3) consistently across both client and server paths, rejecting without silent clamping.
Keep stabilityModes.wsSendMode default 'direct' until rollout evidence supports a default change (Implementation Notes).
Fields without a runtime consumer or with only one literal behavior must stay unavailable/read-only (AC-5) — do not expose literal-only settings as editable.
Large field count (~28 keys): coordinate with FR-BGSTAB-016 and FR-BGSTAB-017, which depend_on these Settings-backed modes/limits.
judgements.external_module_impact: Broad but additive: touches config.schema.ts, RuntimeConfigStore.ts, SettingsService.ts (moderate complexity) plus SettingsPage.tsx UI; risk is breadth (many keys) rather than deep logic in any single hot module.

implementability.rationale: Core change spans wsSendPolicy.ts (~188 LOC/11 branches, small) and WsRouter.ts:1816-2037 (high-complexity module ~2409 LOC/180 branches). Confirmed by direct read: wsSendPolicy.ts ALREADY has lane-separation groundwork (WsTransportQueueState with separate controlItems/terminalItems + controlHead/terminalHead + controlBytes/outputBytes) and same-session coalescing (tryCoalesceOutputMessage guarded by coalesceWindowMs and equal sessionId, no cross-type coalescing). So AC-1 (priority drain control-before-output) and AC-4 (same-session-only coalescing) build directly on existing structures. Remaining work: projected-buffered-byte enforcement (AC-3), priority drain ordering, recovery-transaction barrier (AC-2, must not reorder active recovery transaction), and slow-client handling without session kill (AC-5). The recovery-ordering correctness inside the high-complexity WsRouter is the real cost.
evidence: server/src/ws/wsSendPolicy.ts:12-21 (WsTransportQueueState: separate control/terminal lanes already present)
server/src/ws/wsSendPolicy.ts:49-71 (tryCoalesceOutputMessage: same-session-only coalescing with window guard)
server/src/ws/WsRouter.ts:1816-2037 (priority drain / projected buffer / retry addition_site)
server/src/ws/WsRouter.ts:406-453 (existing snapshot ACK path defining recovery ordering — verifies)

evidence_strength.rationale: wsSendPolicy.ts:1-18 (queue state) and 30-71 (classifier + coalescing) confirmed exactly as described in the trace notes. WsRouter.ts:406-453 (verifies, snapshot ACK path) and 1816-2037/1738-1772 (addition_site) exist (prescreen all_paths_exist=true). Statement and ACs match the addition sites.

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-012 (implemented/evolving, healthy), depends_on FR-BGSTAB-002 (implemented/evolving, healthy), depends_on FR-BGSTAB-015 (planned/evolving — the Settings contract supplying modes/limits). One planned dependency, evolving not draft, no cycle.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (control before unrelated output, no recovery reorder), AC-2 (recovery transaction ordering preserved; input release deferred to FR-BGSTAB-017), AC-3 (projected bufferedAmount+payload vs high/hard limit -> observe/queue/close per mode), AC-4 (same-session coalescing rules, no cross-type coalescing), AC-5 (slow client not killed + telemetry) are all testable via safe-send unit tests, synthetic redraw flood queue tests, and recovery-ordering regression. Clean separation of concerns (input release delegated to FR-017).

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB WebSocket safe-send / backpressure — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Prevents control-traffic starvation under terminal output flood — directly core to 54-session stability, since high-frequency redraw output (the named ConPTY/AI-TUI failure mode) is what starves control traffic and degrades responsiveness at scale.

conditions: depends_on FR-BGSTAB-015 (Settings-backed modes/limits) — sequence after it so mode selection and transport limits are configurable.
Preserve recovery transaction ordering (AC-2) against the existing snapshot ACK path (WsRouter.ts:406-453) — priority drain must not reorder an active recovery transaction.
Keep wsSendMode default 'direct' until observe/enforce evidence supports a default rollout.
Do not make split WebSocket a prerequisite for this requirement.
judgements.external_module_impact: Adds priority-drain and projected-byte logic into WsRouter.ts (high complexity ~2409 LOC/180 branches); wsSendPolicy.ts itself is small and already lane-aware, so the concentrated regression risk is in the WsRouter drain path.

implementability.rationale: Adds a second bounded scheduler (the recovery write gate) alongside the existing live-output scheduler. terminalOutputScheduler.ts (confirmed to expose generalizable flush/shouldYield/takeUtf8Prefix primitives) is generalized with a recovery label/drain/overflow, and the recovery lifecycle is wired across TerminalView.tsx (recoverySchedulerRef + routing snapshot/repair through the gate), TerminalContainer.tsx (ACK-tail routing + input release barrier), and WebSocketContext.tsx (recovery message classification). The scheduler generalization is straightforward (reuse existing primitives); the real complexity is the stateful recovery lifecycle across two very large React components: AC-3 input-release barrier with timeout, AC-5 stale-token callback protection, and AC-6 snapshot-apply-before-ACK ordering. Also co-owns terminalOutputScheduler.ts with PERF-BGSTAB-001.

evidence_strength.rationale: terminalOutputScheduler.ts confirmed to hold the scheduler primitives the REQ generalizes. TerminalView.tsx:1148-1214/1260-1465, TerminalContainer.tsx:1296-1688, WebSocketContext.tsx:141-322 all exist (prescreen all_paths_exist=true, no mismatches). Statement and ACs match the addition sites.

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-003 (implemented/evolving) and FR-BGSTAB-014 (implemented/evolving); depends_on FR-BGSTAB-002 (implemented/evolving, healthy), REL-BGSTAB-003 (planned/evolving), FR-BGSTAB-015 (planned/evolving). Two planned dependencies, both evolving not draft, no cycle. This is the most-dependency-laden of the four re-judged REQs (5 deps).

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (recovery via bounded scheduler not direct write), AC-2 (ACK-tail routed to recovery gate), AC-3 (input release barrier with timeout + observable event), AC-4 (overflow -> stale/recovery-needed, not success), AC-5 (stale-token callbacks cannot mutate current state), AC-6 (snapshot-apply-before-ACK ordering) are all deterministic and testable via recovery scheduler unit tests + TerminalView/TerminalContainer recovery contract tests + WS classification tests, plus Playwright.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB frontend-runtime recovery / input barrier — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Directly addresses input-appears-unavailable during heavy AI TUI redraws — large direct xterm writes for snapshot/repair block the browser main thread. Core to the native-performance / input-lag goal at scale.
evidence: docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1450 (Rationale: large direct writes block main thread, input appears unavailable during heavy AI TUI redraws)

conditions: Co-owns terminalOutputScheduler.ts with PERF-BGSTAB-001 — coordinate implementation order (whichever lands first defines the base).
depends_on FR-BGSTAB-015 (Settings-backed recovery budgets/release timeout) and REL-BGSTAB-003 (both planned) — sequence after them.
Reuse terminalOutputScheduler principles; do not restore the legacy direct-write path as rollback (degraded rollback should lower recovery budgets and force server snapshot refresh on overflow).
Preserve snapshot-apply-before-ACK ordering (AC-6): send screen-snapshot:ready only after the snapshot recovery write completes for the current token.
judgements.external_module_impact: Recovery lifecycle wiring across two very large React components (TerminalView.tsx ~2293 LOC/164 branches, TerminalContainer.tsx ~1878 LOC/130 branches) plus WebSocketContext; stateful ordering/lifecycle changes there carry non-trivial regression risk.

implementability.rationale: Most localized of the four re-judged REQs and the target is precisely identified. Confirmed by direct read: the placeholder is FALLBACK_EMPTY_MESSAGE at TerminalContainer.tsx:131 ('[BuilderGate] Fallback snapshot unavailable. Waiting for new output...'), written through the empty-fallback path at TerminalContainer.tsx:1399-1494 (the 2026-07-01 5-subagent investigation located the write at 1419-1433). The change is a bounded state-machine modification in TerminalContainer: suppress/bound the placeholder write, restore local snapshot when valid (AC-1), preserve buffer + suppress repeated writes per recovery generation (AC-2), still ACK the replay token (AC-3), avoid falsely marking recovery success (AC-4), and reset suppression state on real output / authoritative snapshot / generation change (AC-5). The subtlety is the recovery-accounting guards (AC-4) integrating with existing recovery-pending state; there is a clear existing contract test file to extend.
evidence: frontend/src/components/Terminal/TerminalContainer.tsx:1399-1494 (empty-fallback local restore / placeholder / ACK / recovery-success guards addition_site)
frontend/src/components/Terminal/TerminalView.tsx:1260-1320 (snapshot restore currently writes to xterm — verifies)

evidence_strength.rationale: TerminalContainer.tsx:131 confirmed = the exact FALLBACK_EMPTY_MESSAGE placeholder the REQ targets to suppress/bound. TerminalContainer.tsx:1399-1494 (addition_site), TerminalView.tsx:1260-1320 (verifies), terminalContainerRecoveryContract.test.ts:37-109 (test addition_site) all exist (prescreen all_paths_exist=true). Evidence pedigree strengthened by the documented 5-subagent 2026-07-01 investigation.

dependency_health.rationale: extends FR-BGSTAB-014 (implemented/evolving, healthy); depends_on FR-BGSTAB-017 (planned/evolving — the recovery write gate) and REL-BGSTAB-003 (planned/evolving). Two planned deps, both evolving not draft, no cycle. Notably depends on FR-BGSTAB-017 which itself is planned, so sequencing applies.

ac_verifiability.rationale: No {{OQ}} placeholders. AC-1 (local restore + ACK + no placeholder), AC-2 (repeated-empty suppression + buffer preservation), AC-3 (ACK or observable send-failure so replay is not blocked), AC-4 (recovery-pending preservation guards), AC-5 (suppression reset conditions), AC-6 (deterministic unit tests incl. no raw payload persistence) are all concrete and testable via the existing terminalContainerRecoveryContract.test.ts harness. AC-6 explicitly mandates the deterministic tests.

scope_fit.rationale: BGSTAB frontend-runtime fallback-snapshot / recovery — core stability scope.

product_fit.rationale: Fixes a concrete user-visible defect: the placeholder pollutes scrollback and can reset terminal content during degraded fallback without recovering authoritative output. Core to terminal stability UX.
evidence: docs/research/2026-07-01.buildergate-empty-fallback-snapshot-placeholder-research.md (Related Docs; investigation of the placeholder loop)
docs/spec/30.buildergate-stability.srs.md:1589 (Rationale: placeholder pollutes scrollback and resets content without recovery)

conditions: depends_on FR-BGSTAB-017 (recovery write gate) — sequence after it.

Prefer not writing the empty fallback placeholder into xterm at all; if compatibility requires it, bound to at most one write per recovery generation and keep it out of successful-recovery accounting (AC-2/AC-4).

Preserve replay ACK ordering and recovery-pending semantics — do not clear hidden/visible-output recovery, release queued input, or mark recovery successful solely because placeholder/no-op handling ran (AC-4).
judgements.external_module_impact: Localized change but inside TerminalContainer.tsx (~1878 LOC/130 branches); integrating the empty-fallback suppression with existing recovery-accounting state carries moderate regression risk in a large component.
